description: Conflict detection rules for PolicyPilot. Each rule defines a pair of schemes that may have contradicting eligibility criteria, along with the exact clauses that contradict.

conflictRules.name: Central vs State Agriculture — Land Record Format & Ceiling
conflictRules.triggerCondition: Both schemes matched for the same citizen in agriculture category

centralClause.extractedText: ...cultivable land up to 2 hectares... verified through digitized land records...

stateClause.extractedText: ...offline revenue tokens... No upper ceiling on landholding shall apply.

conflictRules.resolution: Do NOT silently pick one version. Surface both schemes with their original clauses. Guide: if >2 ha, only state scheme applies; if ≤2 ha with digital records, both apply.
conflictRules.detailTemplate:
📄 {centralPdf} → {centralClause}, {centralPage} requires DIGITAL land records and caps land at ≤ 2 hectares.

📄 {statePdf} → {stateClause}, {statePage} accepts OFFLINE revenue tokens and has NO upper land limit.

🚩 CONFLICT: Land record format (Digital vs Offline) and land ceiling (2 ha vs None) directly contradict. If you have > 2 hectares, only the state scheme applies. If you have digital records and ≤ 2 hectares, you qualify for BOTH. The system does NOT silently pick one — both are shown with their original clauses.

translations.hi:
📄 {centralPdf} → कलम {centralClause}, पृष्ठ {centralPage}: डिजिटल भूमि रिकॉर्ड अनिवार्य, भूमि सीमा ≤ 2 हेक्टेयर।
📄 {statePdf} → धारा {stateClause}, पृष्ठ {statePage}: ऑफलाइन टोकन स्वीकृत, कोई भूमि सीमा नहीं।
🚩 विरोधाभास: भूमि रिकॉर्ड प्रारूप और भूमि सीमा में सीधा विरोधाभास।

conflictRules.name: Central MUDRA vs State Business Grant — Margin Money Conflict
conflictRules.triggerCondition: Matched for both PM Mudra and State Business Grants.

centralClause.extractedText: Loans up to ₹10 lakh provided over existing working capital without margin money.

stateClause.extractedText: Financial assistance with 2% interest subvention provided margin is maintained.

conflictRules.resolution: Citizen must declare the Central MUDRA loan while applying for the State Business subvention. You can avail BOTH, but the state will cap the subvention at ₹2 Lakhs.
conflictRules.detailTemplate:
📄 {centralPdf} → {centralClause}, {centralPage}: Provides fully collateral-free loans with NO margin money.

📄 {statePdf} → {stateClause}, {statePage}: Offers 2% interest subvention but requires margin maintenance.

🚩 CONFLICT: Dual funding restriction limits overlapping collateral waivers. 💡 WORKAROUND: You can legally stack both! Just declare your MUDRA loan in the State portal to get the discount applied directly to your MUDRA account.

conflictRules.name: Central Scholarship vs State Scholarship — Double Dipping Prohibition
conflictRules.triggerCondition: Matched for both National Scholarships and State Merit Grants.

centralClause.extractedText: Student shall not hold any other scholarship simultaneously.

stateClause.extractedText: Full tuition waiver is granted irrespective of central funding.

conflictRules.resolution: Legally, a student cannot hold the Central Scholarship and a State Tuition Waiver simultaneously.
conflictRules.detailTemplate:
📄 {centralPdf} → {centralClause}, {centralPage}: Strictly forbids holding multiple scholarships concurrently.

📄 {statePdf} → {stateClause}, {statePage}: Grants 100% full tuition waiver unconditionally.

🚩 CONFLICT: Explicit 'Double Dipping' prohibition. 💡 WORKAROUND: You must choose only ONE. The State framework mathematically provides more savings (100% waiver). Apply for the State scheme and withdraw the National application.
